title: Link disable statement
body:
It fixes linking of `disable` statements.
Disabling blocks referenced with dots works if these blocks aren't inside forks and if they don't contain any. Disabling tasks is still unsupported, but now the appropriate error is thrown.

I think `disable` statement is the only case in which the node can reference a block. I think adding a node like `AstBlockRef` wasn't necessary and we can store a pointer to a disabled block or task directly in `AstDisable` node.
